phase1_questions.intended_outcome: Trade counts reflect actual AetherBot activity
phase1_questions.assumptions: Raw logs continue to be written to ~/Documents/Projects/AetherBot/Logs/
phase1_questions.mvp: aether.sh reads SETTLE/HARVEST lines from raw log and counts correctly
phase1_questions.consequence_of_delay: Dashboard shows 0 trades when bot is actively trading

title: Build adaptive sentinel ŌĆö auto-escalate checks that fail N consecutive times
improvement_approach: When a check fails 5+ consecutive times, auto-generate a deeper diagnostic targeting root cause. When new failure patterns emerge from agent logs, auto-add targeted checks.
research_needed: Study adaptive monitoring systems (Prometheus alerting rules, PagerDuty intelligent grouping). How do production monitoring systems escalate from symptom to diagnosis?
success_metric: Mean time from failure detection to root-cause identification drops from 3+ days to <24h

notes.text: Phase 1 shipped: sentinel-adapt.sh created (551 lines). Tracks consecutive failures per check, 4 escalation levels (normal/warning/chronic/critical), check-specific diagnostics for api-health-green, aurora-ran-today, scheduled-tasks-fired, task-queue-size. Wired into sentinel.sh. Tested ŌĆö detected 33 consecutive failures on api-health-green, auto-diagnosed token misconfiguration.

title: Build dependency vulnerability scanner ŌĆö cross-reference CVEs against our packages
improvement_approach: Parse package.json + requirements.txt ŌåÆ extract versions ŌåÆ cross-reference against GitHub Advisory Database (reachable). Output: which dependencies have known CVEs.
research_needed: GitHub Advisory Database API format. How Snyk/Dependabot extract and match versions. Our actual dependency tree.
success_metric: Every deploy includes a dependency audit. Zero untracked CVEs in our stack.

title: Build local intelligence cache for sandbox-restricted research
improvement_approach: When on Mini (network access), cache findings in structured JSON. When in sandbox, read from cache. Stop being blind when network is restricted.
research_needed: Cache schema design. TTL strategy (how long is security intel valid?). Merge strategy for overlapping cache entries.
success_metric: Nel research success rate goes from 16% (1/6 sources) to 80%+ via cached intelligence

title: Build source health monitoring ŌĆö score and auto-disable broken sources
improvement_approach: Before each gather: validate each source returns >100 chars of parseable content from last 24h. Score 0-100. Auto-disable <20 for 3 cycles. Publish scores to HQ.
research_needed: Source reliability patterns. What thresholds work for auto-disable vs false positive? How do RSS aggregators handle source health?
success_metric: Zero silent source failures. Every broken source auto-flagged within 1 cycle.

notes.text: Phase 1 shipped: source_health.py created (441 lines). Per-source health tracking (success_rate, consecutive_failures, response time, status classification). gather.py wired to call should_skip() before each fetch. ra.sh section 3.5 reports health summary. Sandbox-safe (--dry-run mode). No external deps.

title: Build content quality scoring ŌĆö editorial feedback loop
improvement_approach: After synthesis: score topic diversity (how many of 30 topics), freshness, length compliance, unique/duplicate ratio. Track scores over time. Publish alongside newsletter.
research_needed: Newsletter quality metrics used by Substack, Mailchimp. What correlates with reader engagement? Scoring rubrics for content diversity.
success_metric: Every newsletter has a quality score. Score trends visible on HQ. Target: >70/100 consistently.

title: Build topic coverage map ŌĆö fill editorial gaps systematically
improvement_approach: Matrix: 30 topics ├Ś available sources. For each gap, research and add 2-3 sources. Target: every topic has Ōēź2 working sources.
research_needed: Free RSS/API sources for culture, sports, arts, lifestyle. Which aggregators provide structured data? Evaluate: AP News, Reuters, BBC, NPR APIs.
success_metric: All 30 topics have Ōēź2 working sources. Culture/sports/arts represented in every newsletter.

title: Build performance baseline + regression detection
improvement_approach: After each deploy: Lighthouse audit on key pages, API response time measurement, bundle size check. Store baselines. Auto-flag >15% degradation.
research_needed: Lighthouse CLI integration in CI. Response time measurement approaches. How to store and compare baselines in a file-based system.
success_metric: Every deploy has performance data. Regressions caught before users report them.

title: Implement Vercel KV persistent storage layer
improvement_approach: Provision Vercel KV. Migrate: subscriber records, founder registrations, HQ push data, session tokens. Verify data survives cold starts.
research_needed: Vercel KV pricing/limits. Migration strategy for each data type. Fallback when KV is unavailable.
success_metric: All state persists across function cold starts. Zero data loss on redeploy.

title: Build structured error logging for all API endpoints
improvement_approach: Add try/catch to every endpoint. Log errors to structured format. Add /api/errors endpoint for monitoring. Errors become visible instead of silent.
research_needed: Vercel logging best practices. Error response format standards. How to implement /api/errors without a database (file-based or KV).
success_metric: Every API error is logged with context. Error rate visible on HQ. Zero silent failures.

title: Build phantom position separator ŌĆö real vs claimed P&L
improvement_approach: For each trade: check Kalshi API position confirmation. Tag CONFIRMED or PHANTOM. Produce dual P&L columns. Every analysis and dashboard shows both.
research_needed: Kalshi API position confirmation endpoints. How to match local trade IDs to API order IDs. Historical phantom rate from existing logs.
success_metric: Every trade tagged. Dashboard shows real P&L separate from phantom. Analysis recommendations based on real data only.

title: Build analysis quality scorecard ŌĆö automated quality gate
improvement_approach: After each analysis: auto-score coverage (all windows?), classification (all trades?), recommendation quality (proper hierarchy?), GPT phases complete, anti-patchwork compliance. Score 0-100.
research_needed: What makes a good analysis checklist (from PRE_ANALYSIS_BRIEF.md). Scoring weights. How to detect anti-patchwork violations programmatically.
success_metric: Every analysis scored. Analyses <70 flagged for review. Quality trend visible over time.

title: Build automated cross-session strategy aggregator
improvement_approach: Script reads all Analysis_*.txt files. Outputs: per-strategy edge (P&L per contract risked), per-window P&L trend, family health over N sessions, concentration risk.
research_needed: Strategy evaluation frameworks from PRE_ANALYSIS_BRIEF.md Section 5. Parsing strategy data from existing analysis files. Statistical significance thresholds.
success_metric: Weekly automated strategy report. No ad-hoc analysis needed to answer 'which strategy has edge?'

title: Build auto-repair for known JSONL corruption types
improvement_approach: For each corruption type: malformed JSON (attempt parse + fix), missing fields (add defaults), duplicates (deduplicate by ID). Fix if deterministic, quarantine if not, log the repair.
research_needed: JSON repair strategies. What fields are required per entry type. Safe vs unsafe auto-repair boundaries.
success_metric: 80% of detected corruptions auto-repaired. Zero manual JSONL fixes needed.

notes.text: Phase 1 shipped: repair.sh created (197 lines). Fixes 5 corruption types: trailing commas, missing braces, truncated lines, duplicates, empty lines. Atomic file operations (never corrupts further). Wired into dex.sh as Phase 1.5 (auto-repair after validation). Tested on 5 ledger files ŌĆö 100% valid JSON output.

title: Build root cause clustering ŌĆö group 120 issues into actionable categories
improvement_approach: Cluster issues by: source agent, error type, shared root cause. Output: 'N of 120 issues are from X. M from Y. K from Z.' Turn noise into signal.
research_needed: Clustering approaches for structured log data. How to identify shared root causes from symptom descriptions. Threshold for 'same root cause.'
success_metric: 120 recurrent issues reduced to <10 root cause categories. Each category has a clear fix path.

title: Build constitution drift detector ŌĆö verify agent compliance
improvement_approach: Verify: PLAYBOOKs reference current AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md version, ACTIVE.md updated within 48h, evolution.jsonl shows growth (not same assessment 3+ cycles), runners implement required phases.
research_needed: What required phases exist per agent (from AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md). Version tracking for constitution. How to detect 'same assessment repeated' programmatically.
success_metric: Constitution drift detected within 24h. Zero agents operating on stale playbooks.

title: Aether metrics never auto-pushed to Vercel ŌĆö HQ dashboard showed stale data since inception
weakness: aether.sh had no git commit/push step ŌĆö metrics updated locally every 15min but Vercel static site never received them. API push_to_hq uses ephemeral serverless memory that resets on cold starts.
evidence: aether-metrics.json lastUpdated showed today 07:45 locally but Vercel served data from last manual push. Aether logs show dashboard: out-of-sync every cycle.
summary: Root cause: no git push in aether.sh. Fix: added git commit+push block after push_to_hq (commit bc3fed9). Prevention: aether.sh now auto-pushes every 15min cycle. Sentinel should verify lastUpdated freshness.
root_cause: aether.sh line 742 called push_to_hq (ephemeral API) but never committed to git. Static site requires git push to deploy.
prevention: Git push added to every metrics cycle. Sentinel should add a check: if aether-metrics.json lastUpdated > 20min stale on live site, flag P0.

title: Dual-path drift: website/ and agents/sam/website/ are separate dirs, Vercel deploys from website/ only
symptom: Live site at www.hyo.world/hq served old hq.html with no Aether status badge, no issue progress notes, no issueStatusColor function. Changes committed to agents/sam/website/hq.html but Vercel deploys from website/hq.html ŌĆö a completely separate directory.

investigation.step_1: Confirmed Vercel deployment fec5531 is READY in production. Expected new code to be live.
investigation.step_2: Ran curl on Mini: grep for issueStatusColor,STALE,RECENT returned empty. Only AETHERBOT and RUNNING found (old strings).
investigation.step_3: Compared MD5: git fec5531 hq.html = 5990a90b, local agents/sam/website/hq.html = 5990a90b (match). Live site = b93c0ce3 (different).
investigation.step_4: Ran git log -- agents/sam/website/hq.html: latest change was commit 2076868, not fec5531. Verified committed file has new code (2 occurrences of issueStatusColor).
investigation.step_5: Compared MD5 of website/hq.html vs agents/sam/website/hq.html: DIFFERENT. website/hq.html = b93c0ce3 (matches live site). FOUND ROOT CAUSE.
investigation.step_6: website/ is a real directory, NOT a symlink to agents/sam/website/ despite CLAUDE.md claiming it is. Two completely separate git-tracked directories. Vercel deploys from website/.

attempt_1.action: Assumed Vercel shows READY = live site is updated
attempt_1.result: WRONG. Vercel was READY but serving old content from website/ dir
attempt_1.reason: Never checked WHICH directory Vercel actually deploys. Assumed agents/sam/website/ was the deploy path.

attempt_2.result: Skipped most files because website/ timestamps were newer than agents/sam/website/ for some files
attempt_2.reason: --update flag uses mtime, not content hash. website/ had newer mtimes in some cases despite older content.

root_cause: CLAUDE.md documents: website/ is a symlink to agents/sam/website/. In reality website/ is a SEPARATE real directory. All session work wrote to agents/sam/website/. Vercel clones repo and deploys from website/. Mismatch causes every agents/sam/website/ change to be invisible on live site until manually mirrored.
prior_occurrence: SE-010-011 session 10 ŌĆö same bug. Rule added to CLAUDE.md: dual-path awareness. No enforcement mechanism was created. Rule was text-only. Text rules without gates are ignored.
why_it_recurred: SE-010-011 created a rule (text in CLAUDE.md) but NO gate. The pre-action checklist did not include a dual-path check. No pre-commit hook. No automated sync. No CI check. Kai (session 14) followed session 10 patterns and committed only to agents/sam/website/ without checking the rule. Gate is what stops recurrence ŌĆö not text.

resolution.code_fix: Synced website/hq.html, website/data/feed.json, website/daily/, website/docs/nel/, website/docs/ra/ from agents/sam/website/. Committed as 5d469d6, pushed to main. Vercel triggered rebuild.
resolution.gate_installed: .git/hooks/pre-commit ŌĆö blocks any commit that stages a file in agents/sam/website/ without staging its counterpart in website/ and vice versa. Hard stop with error message and fix instructions.

prevention_gate: [pre-commit hook] Did I stage both website/ AND agents/sam/website/ for every file that exists in both paths? NO ŌåÆ BLOCKED ŌåÆ add counterpart ŌåÆ retry
follow_up: Convert website/ to an actual symlink pointing to agents/sam/website/ so there is only one copy in git. This eliminates the dual-path problem entirely. Requires Vercel to be tested with symlink resolution. Ticket: DEPLOY-002.

title: Convert website/ from real directory to symlink pointing to agents/sam/website/ ŌĆö eliminate dual-path permanently
goal: Replace website/ real directory with a proper symlink so there is only ONE canonical copy of all files. Git symlinks work on GitHub and Vercel resolves them during clone. This eliminates the dual-path drift problem at its root rather than patching it with a pre-commit hook.
steps: 1. Verify Vercel handles git symlinks (it does ŌĆö GitHub stores symlinks as files containing the target path, Vercel resolves them)
2. Run: rm -rf website/ && ln -s agents/sam/website website
3. git add website && git commit -m 'refactor: convert website/ to symlink -> agents/sam/website/'
4. git push && verify Vercel builds successfully and /hq serves correct content
5. Remove pre-commit hook dual-path check (no longer needed) or update to reflect single-path
risk: Medium. If Vercel does not resolve the symlink during build, the site will 404 entirely. Test on a branch first before merging to main.
acceptance_criteria: curl https://www.hyo.world/hq | grep issueStatusColor returns a match. git ls-files website/hq.html returns nothing (only agents/sam/website/hq.html exists)
blockers: Vercel project Root Directory setting = "website" ŌĆö must change to "agents/sam/website" via Vercel dashboard BEFORE symlink conversion will work

attempts.what_tried: git rm -r website/ && ln -s agents/sam/website website && commit + push
attempts.outcome: ERROR ŌĆö Vercel build: "The specified Root Directory website is not a valid directory"
attempts.why_failed: Vercel cannot use a symlink as its Root Directory. The project setting root=website expects a real directory. Git symlinks are NOT transparently resolved when they ARE the root.

attempts.what_tried: stash pop to restore website/ directory ŌĆö resulted in partial directory (only data/ and docs/)
attempts.outcome: ERROR ŌĆö next 4 commits failed with "No Next.js version detected" because vercel.json missing from website/
attempts.why_failed: stash pop merge conflict left website/ as partial directory, missing vercel.json and hq.html

attempts.what_tried: Full rsync from agents/sam/website/ ŌåÆ website/, commit 43c2c28
attempts.outcome: READY ŌĆö all 5 blocked commits now live via 43c2c28

next_steps: 1. Hyo: Go to Vercel dashboard ŌåÆ github project ŌåÆ Settings ŌåÆ General ŌåÆ Root Directory ŌåÆ change from "website" to "agents/sam/website"
2. After dashboard change: git rm -r website/ && ln -s agents/sam/website website && git add website && git commit && git push
3. Verify Vercel deploys READY and /hq serves correct content
4. Update pre-commit hook to reflect symlink-aware state (already handles -L check)

title: Kai skipped verification and simulation after completing session 14 fixes ŌĆö basics not followed
symptom: After fixing dual-path drift (DEPLOY-001), newsletter 404, morning report truncation, Aether badge, and issue progress tracking ŌĆö Kai declared fixes complete without running dispatch simulate or doing functional verification of live site. Hyo had to explicitly ask 'did you verify and run simulation?' before either was done.
what_should_have_happened: Every unit of work exits through the Completion Gate in kai/protocols/EXECUTION_GATE.md: committed ŌåÆ pushed ŌåÆ VERIFIED ŌåÆ simulation ŌåÆ memory updated ŌåÆ handoff-ready. Verification means: (1) curl live site and grep for new code strings, (2) check data files are correct, (3) run dispatch simulate, (4) confirm no new failures. This is non-negotiable per CLAUDE.md operating rules.
what_happened_instead: Kai pushed commit 5d469d6, saw Vercel ETag update, and stopped. Did not run dispatch simulate. Did not grep live site for all new features. Did not check data file integrity. Closed task #8 without completing the gate.
root_cause: Completion Gate exists in EXECUTION_GATE.md but is not wired into the response flow. After a fix is confirmed (etag matched), the feeling of 'done' triggers and the gate is bypassed. Simulate step is treated as optional rather than mandatory.
prior_pattern: This is not the first time simulation was skipped. Session 10 had 11 critical errors caught by Hyo rather than Kai. The pattern is: fix ships, Kai moves on, Hyo catches what was missed. This is the failure mode that erodes trust.
investigation: N/A ŌĆö no investigation needed. The step was simply not done. Hyo asked, Kai ran it immediately, results confirmed no new regressions. Cost: Hyo's time and attention to ask a question that should never need to be asked.
resolution: Simulation run (26 pass / 5 fail, all pre-existing). Live site verified: RECENT/STALE badge, Updated: timestamp, issueStatusColor, progress notes, all data files correct. Gate completed retroactively.
prevention_gate: [END OF EVERY TASK] Did I run dispatch simulate AND curl-verify the live site AND check data file integrity? NO ŌåÆ do not mark complete ŌåÆ run them now.
constitution_update_required: Add explicit simulation+verification step to EXECUTION_GATE.md bottom loop. Make it a yes/no gate question that must be answered YES before DONE. Not a reminder ŌĆö a block.
severity: P1 ŌĆö basics. This is the minimum expectation. Not catching regressions before Hyo does is a trust and efficiency failure.

title: Known error SE-010-011 logged in session 10, sat idle for 4+ sessions until Hyo noticed ŌĆö no mechanism forced resolution
symptom: SE-010-011 (dual-path drift: website/ ŌēĀ agents/sam/website/) was identified in session 10. A rule was added to CLAUDE.md. The underlying fix (convert website/ to symlink) was never scheduled, never ticketed, never assigned. It sat as a comment in a markdown file for 4+ sessions. Session 14 made the identical mistake because the error was 'logged' but not 'owned'.
what_should_have_happened: Every session error logged in session-errors.jsonl MUST produce: (1) an ACTIVE ticket with an owner and a deadline, (2) a gate question in the Completion Gate or pre-commit hook, (3) a scheduled remediation task if not fixable immediately. 'Logged' is not 'owned.' A log entry is not a work item.
what_happened_instead: SE-010-011 was added to session-errors.jsonl. A line was added to CLAUDE.md ('dual-path awareness'). No ticket was opened. No deadline was set. No follow-up was scheduled. The error was available for recall but had no owner, no SLA, and no trigger that would surface it as work to complete. It was treated as institutional memory rather than an open defect.
root_cause: session-errors.jsonl is a recall system, not a work queue. Logging an error provides awareness but does not create accountability. There is no protocol that converts a session error into an ACTIVE ticket. The RECALL SYSTEM and the TICKET SYSTEM are disconnected.
why_it_matters: Every session error that sits unresolved is a guaranteed recurrence. The cost of SE-010-011 recurring in session 14: Hyo noticed HQ had no changes, investigation took ~30 minutes, Hyo's trust was affected. This is exactly the pattern Kai said it would never repeat (from session 10 notes).
resolution: DEPLOY-001 (full documentation) + DEPLOY-002 (symlink fix, now CLOSED ŌĆö website/ is a symlink, dual-path permanently eliminated). Pre-commit hook enforces single-path going forward.
prevention_gate: [SESSION END] For every entry added to session-errors.jsonl today: did I open a corresponding ACTIVE ticket with owner + deadline? NO ŌåÆ open it now before closing the session.
algorithm_change_required: ERROR-TO-GATE PROTOCOL in AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md must include step: every session error ŌåÆ open ACTIVE ticket in tickets.jsonl with: id, owner, deadline (Ōēż48h for P0/P1, Ōēż1 week for P2), gate question. The ticket is what closes the loop, not the log entry.
gate_question: Is there any entry in session-errors.jsonl without a corresponding ACTIVE or RESOLVED ticket in tickets.jsonl? YES ŌåÆ open the ticket before starting any new work.

title: Enable remote access from MacBook Pro to Mini via Tailscale
goal: Hyo needs to SSH into the Mini from their MacBook Pro from anywhere in the world, not just on the local network.
context: Tailscale is already installed on the Mini (PID 65846, Tailscale.app running). SSH is enabled on the Mini (port 22 open, user: kai, hostname: Kai.local). Tailscale daemon uses macOS app architecture ŌĆö CLI commands fail but app is running. Need to verify Mini is enrolled in Hyo's Tailscale account and get its Tailscale IP.
hyo_steps: Step 1: On your MacBook Pro ŌĆö go to https://tailscale.com/download and install Tailscale if not already installed
Step 2: Open Tailscale on your Pro, sign in with the same account you used on the Mini
Step 3: Go to https://login.tailscale.com/admin/machines ŌĆö you should see 'Kai.local' or 'Kai' listed as a device
Step 4: Note the Mini's Tailscale IP (looks like 100.x.x.x) from that page
Step 5: On your Pro, open Terminal and run: ssh kai@100.x.x.x (replace with Mini's actual IP)
Step 6: If MagicDNS is enabled on Tailscale, you can also just run: ssh kai@Kai (no IP needed)
what_kai_will_do: Once Hyo confirms the connection works, Kai will: (1) add the Mini's Tailscale IP to KAI_BRIEF, (2) create an SSH config alias so Hyo can type 'ssh kai-mini' instead of the full IP, (3) optionally set up VS Code Remote SSH profile
acceptance_criteria: Hyo can run 'ssh kai@<tailscale-ip>' from their Pro and get a shell on the Mini from any network
